chore(node): update beads issue tracker state

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -362,6 +362,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-1wqfg","title":"Reject internally inconsistent incident replay bundles","description":"Code archaeology in crates/franken-node/src/tools/replay_bundle.rs found that validate_bundle_integrity() only recomputes the top-level integrity hash over the serialized bundle. It does not verify that manifest metadata and chunk layout are still the deterministic derivations of the authoritative timeline. As a result, a self-hashed but internally malformed bundle can make incident replay consume chunk events while counterfactual replay consumes timeline events, producing workflow divergence from the same artifact. Tighten bundle validation so manifest/chunks must exactly match the deterministic derivation from timeline + policy/state, then add regressions for chunk-layout drift and manifest drift.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-29T04:06:50.667911407Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-29T04:23:25.660069141Z","closed_at":"2026-03-29T04:23:25.660047019Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-1wz","title":"[PLAN 10.17] Radical Expansion Execution Track (9K)","description":"Section: 10.17 — Radical Expansion Execution Track (9K)

Strategic Context:
Radical expansion track for proof-carrying speculation, Bayesian adversary control, time-travel replay, ZK attestations, and claim compiler systems.

Execution Requirements:
- Preserve all scoped capabilities from the canonical plan.
- Keep contracts self-contained so future contributors can execute without reopening the master plan.
- Require explicit testing strategy (unit + integration/e2e) and structured logging/telemetry for every child bead.
- Require artifact-backed validation for performance, security, and correctness claims.

Dependency Semantics:
- This epic is blocked by its child implementation beads.
- Cross-epic dependencies encode strategic sequencing and canonical ownership boundaries.

## Success Criteria
- All child beads for this section are completed with acceptance artifacts attached and no unresolved blockers.
- Section-level verification gate for comprehensive unit tests, integration/e2e workflows, and detailed structured logging evidence is green.
- Scope-to-plan traceability is explicit, with no feature loss versus the canonical plan section.

## Optimization Notes
- User-Outcome Lens: "[PLAN 10.17] Radical Expansion Execution Track (9K)" must improve operator confidence, safety posture, and deterministic recovery behavior under both normal and adversarial conditions.
- Cross-Section Coordination: child beads must encode integration assumptions explicitly to avoid local optimizations that degrade system-wide correctness.
- Verification Non-Negotiable: completion requires reproducible unit/integration/E2E evidence and structured logs suitable for independent replay.
- Scope Protection: any simplification must preserve canonical-plan feature intent and be justified with explicit tradeoff documentation.","notes":"Acceptance Criteria alias (plan-space normalization, 2026-02-20):
- The `Success Criteria` section in this bead is the canonical acceptance contract for closure.
- Closure additionally requires linked unit/integration/E2E verification evidence and detailed structured logging artifacts from all child implementation +{"id":"bd-1wzk9","title":"Harden check_network_guard verification script for repo execution policy","description":"`scripts/check_network_guard.py` has two concrete repo-policy problems discovered during archaeology/audit:

- it opens `network_guard_audit_samples.jsonl` with bare `open(...).readlines()` instead of a context manager
- it invokes `cargo test` directly instead of routing the cargo-heavy check through `rch exec --`, which violates the repository build-offload rule in `AGENTS.md`

Scope:
- switch the audit JSONL read to a context-managed read path
- route the Rust test invocation through `rch exec -- cargo test ...`
- add/update unit coverage for the command construction and audit-sample parsing behavior

Acceptance:
- no bare file-handle leak remains in `scripts/check_network_guard.py`
- script uses `rch exec --` for its cargo-backed verification step
- `tests/test_check_network_guard.py` passes
- `ubs scripts/check_network_guard.py tests/test_check_network_guard.py` is clean enough for this touched surface","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"AmberRidge","created_at":"2026-04-02T03:36:20.163618315Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T03:55:03.778692142Z","closed_at":"2026-04-02T03:55:03.778665402Z","close_reason":"Completed: context-managed audit reads, rch-backed cargo test invocation, helper coverage, and verification","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","tooling","verification"]}
 {"id":"bd-1x3v2","title":"IdempotencyDedupeStore.entries BTreeMap grew without capacity limit. Added MAX_DEDUPE_ENTRIES=65536; when at capacity, expired entries are swept first, then new inserts rejected if still full.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-03-22T16:20:43.767116497Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T16:20:51.741381981Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T16:20:51.741353909Z","close_reason":"Fixed: MAX_DEDUPE_ENTRIES=65536 with sweep-then-reject","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1xao","title":"[13] Success criterion: impossible-by-default adoption","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 13

Task Objective:
Track and gate production-grade adoption of impossible-by-default capabilities.

Execution Requirements:
- Make this requirement machine-verifiable and release-gated where applicable.
- Keep behavior self-documenting so future contributors do not need to re-open the master plan.

Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for rule/contract logic and error conditions.
- Integration/E2E tests for workflow-level enforcement.
- Structured logs with stable codes and trace IDs.
- Reproducible artifacts that prove contract satisfaction.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Success and failure invariants for [13] Success criterion: impossible-by-default adoption are explicitly quantified and machine-verifiable.
- Determinism requirements for [13] Success criterion: impossible-by-default adoption are validated across repeated runs and adversarial perturbations.


Expected Artifacts:
- Machine-readable verification artifact with explicit canonical path under artifacts/section_13/bd-1xao/verification_evidence.json, suitable for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary with explicit canonical path under artifacts/section_13/bd-1xao/verification_summary.md, linking implementation intent to measured validation outcomes.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- The implementation must deliver the exact capability named in the title, with no scope dilution and no silent feature omission.
- Validation artifacts must include capability-specific thresholds, pass/fail criteria, and reproducible evidence bundles tied to this bead ID.
- Program/section verification gates must be able to consume this bead's outputs without manual interpretation.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[13] Success criterion: impossible-by-default adoption" is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[13] Success criterion: impossible-by-default adoption" prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","acceptance_criteria":"1. 'Impossible-by-default' capabilities are defined: operations that are blocked unless explicitly enabled with cryptographic authorization.
2. At minimum, these capabilities are impossible-by-default: (a) arbitrary file system access outside project root, (b) outbound network to non-allowlisted hosts, (c) spawning child processes without sandbox, (d) loading unsigned extensions, (e) disabling hardening profiles.
3. Each impossible-by-default capability requires explicit opt-in via signed capability token with expiry.
4. Attempting a blocked operation produces a clear, actionable error message (not a generic permission denied).
5. Adoption metric: >= 90% of production deployments run with all impossible-by-default capabilities enforced (measured via telemetry).
6. No impossible-by-default capability can be silently disabled; any disabling is logged and alerted.
7. Evidence: capability_enforcement_report.json listing each capability, enforcement status, and opt-in rate.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:39:34.668279352Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T23:37:02.590071518Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T23:37:02.590043385Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-13","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1xao","depends_on_id":"bd-pga7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:55:04Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1xbc","title":"[10.17] Add deterministic time-travel runtime capture/replay for extension-host workflows.","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 10.17 — Radical Expansion Execution Track (9K)

Why This Exists:
Radical expansion track for proof-carrying speculation, Bayesian adversary control, time-travel replay, ZK attestations, and claim compiler systems.

Task Objective:
Add deterministic time-travel runtime capture/replay for extension-host workflows.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Captured executions replay byte-for-byte equivalent control decisions under same seed/input; incident replay includes stepwise state navigation and divergence explanation.

Expected Artifacts:
- `docs/specs/time_travel_runtime.md`, `src/replay/time_travel_engine.rs`, `tests/lab/time_travel_replay_equivalence.rs`, `artifacts/10.17/time_travel_replay_report.json`.

- Machine-readable verification artifact at `artifacts/section_10_17/bd-1xbc/verification_evidence.json` for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary at `artifacts/section_10_17/bd-1xbc/verification_summary.md` linking implementation intent to measured outcomes.
Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for core logic, invariants, and error handling.
- Integration/E2E tests for end-to-end control-flow behavior.
- Detailed structured logs/telemetry with stable codes and trace correlation IDs.
- Deterministic replay fixture or reproducible artifact for failure analysis.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- For "[10.17] Add deterministic time-travel runtime capture/replay for extension-host workflows.", preserve full capability scope from the plan; no feature compression or silent omission is allowed.
- Verification for "[10.17] Add deterministic time-travel runtime capture/replay for extension-host workflows." must include capability-specific thresholds and machine-readable pass/fail evidence artifacts.
- Unit tests, integration/E2E scripts, and structured logs for "[10.17] Add deterministic time-travel runtime capture/replay for extension-host workflows." must support deterministic replay and root-cause triage without hidden context.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[10.17] Add deterministic time-travel runtime capture/replay for extension-host workflows." is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[10.17] Add deterministic time-travel runtime capture/replay for extension-host workflows." prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","acceptance_criteria":"- Captured executions replay byte-for-byte equivalent control decisions under same seed/input; incident replay includes stepwise state navigation and divergence explanation.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:37:03.099102362Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T05:29:57.447111352Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T05:29:57.447089121Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-17","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1xbc","depends_on_id":"bd-274s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:55:04Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Section: 10.13 — FCP Deep-Mined Expansion Execution Track (9I)

 {"id":"bd-2ke","title":"[PLAN 14] Benchmark + Standardization Ownership","description":"Section 14 standards epic. Publish benchmark specs/harnesses/datasets/scoring, include security+trust co-metrics, ship verifier toolkit, and version standards with migration guidance.

## Success Criteria
- All child beads for this section are completed with acceptance artifacts attached and no unresolved blockers.
- Section-level verification gate for comprehensive unit tests, integration/e2e workflows, and detailed structured logging evidence is green.
- Scope-to-plan traceability is explicit, with no feature loss versus the canonical plan section.

Section: 10.2 — Compatibility Core

Why This Exists:
Compatibility core as strategic wedge: policy-visible behavior, divergence receipts, L1/L2 lockstep, and spec-first fixture governance.

Task Objective:
Implement compatibility mode selection policy (`strict`, `balanced`, `legacy-risky`).

Acceptance Criteria:
- Implement with explicit success/failure invariants and deterministic behavior under normal, edge, and fault scenarios.
- Ensure outcomes are verifiable via automated tests and reproducible artifact outputs.

Expected Artifacts:
- Section-owned design/spec artifact at `docs/specs/section_10_2/bd-2kf_contract.md`, capturing decision rationale, invariants, and interface boundaries.
- Machine-readable verification artifact at `artifacts/section_10_2/bd-2kf/verification_evidence.json` for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary at `artifacts/section_10_2/bd-2kf/verification_summary.md` linking implementation intent to measured outcomes.

Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for core logic, invariants, and error handling.
- Integration/E2E tests for end-to-end control-flow behavior.
- Detailed structured logs/telemetry with stable codes and trace correlation IDs.
- Deterministic replay fixture or reproducible artifact for failure analysis.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- For "[10.2] Implement compatibility mode selection policy (`strict`, `balanced`, `legacy-risky`).", preserve full capability scope from the plan; no feature compression or silent omission is allowed.
- Verification for "[10.2] Implement compatibility mode selection policy (`strict`, `balanced`, `legacy-risky`)." must include capability-specific thresholds and machine-readable pass/fail evidence artifacts.
- Unit tests, integration/E2E scripts, and structured logs for "[10.2] Implement compatibility mode selection policy (`strict`, `balanced`, `legacy-risky`)." must support deterministic replay and root-cause triage without hidden context.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[10.2] Implement compatibility mode selection policy (`strict`, `balanced`, `legacy-risky`)." is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[10.2] Implement compatibility mode selection policy (`strict`, `balanced`, `legacy-risky`)." prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:36:44.077237537Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T09:38:09.529574909Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T09:38:09.529546275Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-2","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2kf","depends_on_id":"bd-38l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:55:04Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
